title: idle drain product waits H2
purpose: D3.4 defines idle drain product waits h2 for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D3.4` only; siblings under `D3` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: When [A4.1](A4.1-stop-human-h1-h2-h3.md) classifies pursuit as blocked on external H2—credentials, legal approval, operator answer—check-pipeline-blocked holds product `next_action` while conductor attention would otherwise idle.

Idle drain routes autopilot or /loop turns to dequeue `state.platform.promotion_queue` items—playbook-keeper, script extraction, catalog rows—without advancing product `next_action` or clearing `gates_pending`.
Platform workers run under conductor spawn contracts with queue item ids; they may not dual-write consumer pursuit state, waive H2, or mark implement tasks complete during idle drain.
When the operator clears H2, pursuit resumes the frozen `next_action` and product lanes; journal records how many platform items drained during the wait.
If idle drain advances product scope, clears `blocking_questions`, or fakes implement progress while H2 remains open, validate-workflow fails closed and pursuit reverts to the prior blocked state.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Platform queue depth 0 but product blocked on missing playbook â€” D3.3 priority cut skips platform drain.
Edge case `D3.4` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Pass 3: add regression test or evidence path specific to `D3.4`.
Pass 3: cross-link related nodes in same branch index.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.
implementation: Scheduler in autopilot workflow: `(steps_total % K) == 0` â†’ platform turn.
playbook-keeper + script extraction skills dequeue promotion items.
Validate `D3.4` against SEC-15 release checklist and parent index links.
Document `D3.4` in parent index with verify command and release tag.
Add checklist row in SEC-15 release doc for `D3.4`.
acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: D3.4 defines idle drain product waits h2 for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system when [a4.1](a4.1-stop-human-h1-h2-h3.md) classifies pursuit as blocked on external h2—credentials, legal approval, operator answer—check-pipeline-blocked holds product `next_action` while conductor attention would otherwise idle, then idle drain routes autopilot or /loop turns to dequeue `state.platform.promotion_queue` items—playbook-keeper, script extraction, catalog rows—without advancing product `next_action` or clearing `gates_pending`.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

When product pursuit waits on external H2—credentials, legal approval, operator answer—CPU and conductor attention would otherwise idle. Idle drain spends that wait on platform queue processing: dequeue and promote without advancing product next_action or clearing gates.

Idle drain respects H2 semantics: no silent gate clearance, no fake implement progress. It maximizes parallel platform evolution during human latency and pairs naturally with autopilot loops that would otherwise spin on blocked checks. When H2 clears, product turns resume on prior next_action; platform backlog should be thinner than if waits were pure no-ops. See [A4.1](A4.1-stop-human-h1-h2-h3.md) stop taxonomy.
